description: Reader reads input, tilize(RM)/direct(TILE), identity copy c1->c16, untilize(RM)/direct(TILE), writer outputs

approach: RM path: batch-32 stick reads into c_0 with TensorAccessor, TILE path: per-tile reads into c_1. Generates scaler in c_2 and epsilon in c_5 via prepare_reduce_scaler.
RM: tilize c0->c1, copy_tiles c1->c16, untilize c16->c17. TILE: copy_tiles c1->c16. Uses helpers for all phases.
RM: wait Wt pages in c17, extract 32 sticks per row, write via TensorAccessor. TILE: wait 1 tile in c16, write via TensorAccessor.

cb_summary: RM: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt(copy) c16:Wt/Wt c17:Wt/Wt. TILE: c1:1x_Wt/1x_Wt c16:1x_Wt/1x_Wt

description: Square(c1->c3), reduce_sum_row(c3->c4 with c2 scaler), add_eps(c4+c5->c6)+rsqrt post-op. Output c6->c16 for reduced shape.

change: Changed output shape to reduced W=32 for intermediate stage 2
reason: Stage 2 outputs rsqrt(mean(x^2)+eps) which has reduced W dimension

change: Writer stick_size now computed from output_tensor shape, not input_tensor. Writer Wt is output_Wt.
reason: Stage 2 has reduced output W=32, writer must use output dimensions.

approach: Added square(c1->c3), reduce_row(c3->c4 with c2), add_eps+rsqrt(c4+c5->c6), copy(c6->c16), untilize 1 tile. WaitAndPopPerTile on c1 since no reuse in this stage.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt(sq) c3:Wt/Wt(reduce) c2:1/persist c4:1/1(add) c5:1/persist c6:1/1(copy) c16:1/1 c17:1/1

details: TypeError: Shape.__getitem__ incompatible with slice. Host-side error in entry point or program descriptor shape indexing.

description: ttnn.Shape does not support slice indexing shape[:-1]. Need to convert to list first before slicing in entry point.
evidence: TypeError: __getitem__() incompatible with slice on ttnn.Shape

change: Convert ttnn.Shape to list first, then slice for output_shape computation

details: Exit code 2 on 1x1x64x128 TILE (multi-core). First test 1x1x32x32 passed. dispatch_kernel_initializer crash - likely writer start_id computed with input Wt instead of output_Wt.

description: Writer start_id for TILE path uses input Wt for tile offset but output tensor has output_Wt=1. Need separate writer start_id computation using output_Wt. Also RM writer start_stick_id uses same start as reader but output sticks are shorter.
evidence: 1x1x32x32 (single core, Wt=1) passes but 1x1x64x128 (multi-core, Wt=4) crashes at dispatch

change: Split reader and writer start_id computation. Writer TILE path uses output_Wt for tile offset, reader uses input Wt.
reason: Output tensor has reduced W=32 (output_Wt=1), writer tile IDs must match output layout not input layout.

change: Writer start_id now uses output_Wt for TILE path tile offset computation

triage: TRISC2(pack) stuck at cb_reserve_back(c3) in square. TRISC0(unpack) stuck at cb_wait_front(c3) in reduce. c3 has 2 pages but square tries to push Wt=4 tiles before reduce starts.
suspected_cause: Sequential square then reduce with only 2 pages in c3. Square fills c3 before reduce starts consuming.

description: c3 CB has only 2 pages but square and reduce are sequential calls. Square pushes Wt tiles (e.g. 4 for W=128) to c3 before reduce starts consuming. Pack thread blocks at cb_reserve_back after 2 tiles. Fix: increase c3 to Wt pages.
evidence: TRISC2 stuck at cb_reserve_back in square, TRISC0 stuck at cb_wait_front in reduce. Both on c3.

change: Increased c3 (cb_sq) from 2 pages to Wt pages
reason: Square and reduce are sequential calls. Square must push all Wt tiles before reduce starts consuming. With 2 pages, pack thread blocks after 2 tiles.

change: c3 CB size increased from 2 to Wt pages to hold all squared tiles before reduce consumes them

upstream_fixes: Fixed ttnn.Shape slice indexing in entry point
Split writer start_id to use output_Wt
Increased c3 from 2 to Wt pages

description: Add mul_bcast_col phase: x * rsqrt(mean(x^2)+eps). Persist c_1 via WaitUpfrontNoPop in square, NoWaitNoPop in mul. Full output shape restored.

change: Restored output shape to match input (reverted reduced W=32 from stage 2)

approach: Changed square to WaitUpfrontNoPop on c1 to persist. Replaced copy c6->c16 with mul<COL>(c1,c6)->c16. Added manual cb_pop_front(c1,Wt) after mul. Untilize restored to Wt width.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/waitUpfront+noWaitNoPop+manualPop c3:Wt/Wt c2:persist c4:1/1 c5:persist c6:1/1(COL) c16:Wt/Wt c17:Wt/Wt

upstream_fixes: Restored output shape to full input dimensions

description: Add gamma multiply phase: mul<ROW>(c_16*c_7)->c_4. Reader loads gamma sticks into c_0, compute tilizes gamma into c_7. Writer reads from c_4 when gamma present.

approach: Added gamma TensorAccessor CT args after input accessor. Before main loop, if has_gamma: reserve c_0 Wt pages, read 1 gamma stick (stick_id=0) into row 0, push Wt pages. ROW broadcast only uses row 0 so rows 1-31 garbage is fine.

Added cb_gamma=7. Pre-loop: tilize gamma c_0->c_7 when has_gamma. Phase 6: mul<ROW>(c_out, c_gamma, cb_rms) WaitAndPopPerTile on c_out, WaitUpfrontNoPop on c_gamma. Phase 7 untilize reads from cb_rms when gamma, cb_out when no gamma. Pop c_7 at end of kernel.
Added cb_rms=4, cb_tile_source constexpr selecting c_4 when has_gamma else c_16. TILE path now reads from cb_tile_source. RM path unchanged (always c_17).

change: c_0 pages: Wt if (is_rm or has_gamma) else 1
reason: Gamma loading always uses c_0 even for TILE path - needs Wt pages for gamma stick tilize

change: c_4 pages: Wt if has_gamma else 2
reason: c_4 is reused as gamma multiply output (Wt tiles). With only 2 pages, untilize WaitBlock on Wt tiles would deadlock.

change: Added gamma TensorAccessorArgs CT args after input accessor args when has_gamma
reason: Reader kernel uses TensorAccessorArgs<next_offset>() to create gamma accessor for DRAM reads

approach: Moved gamma_accessor_args inside if constexpr (has_gamma) block to avoid compile error when gamma CT args not present. TensorAccessorArgs would read nonexistent CT args otherwise.

change: Added gamma_stick_size CT arg at index 6, shifted TensorAccessorArgs to start at index 7
reason: TILE path sets stick_or_tile_size=tile_size but gamma is always RM with page_size=stick_size. Gamma accessor needs correct page size.

approach: Added gamma_stick_size CT arg at index 6, shifted input TensorAccessorArgs to start at index 7. This ensures gamma accessor uses correct RM page size even when input is TILE path.
Changed gamma accessor and noc_async_read to use gamma_stick_size instead of stick_or_tile_size for correct RM page size.

summary: RM+gamma: reader pushes c_0(Wt) for gamma pre-loop, compute tilize c_0->c_7(Wt) pre-loop. Main loop: reader pushes c_0(Wt), P1 tilize pops c_0 pushes c_1(Wt), P2 square WaitUpfrontNoPop c_1 pushes c_3(Wt), P3 reduce pops c_3 pushes c_4(1), P4 add pops c_4 pushes c_6(1), P5 mul NoWaitNoPop c_1 pops c_6 pushes c_16(Wt), manual pop c_1(Wt), P6 gamma mul pops c_16 WaitUpfrontNoPop c_7 pushes c_4(Wt), P7 untilize waits c_4(Wt) pops c_4 pushes c_17(Wt), writer pops c_17(Wt). End: pop c_7(Wt). TILE+gamma: same compute phases, writer reads c_4 tile-by-tile. All balanced.
Device init failure: Timeout waiting for Ethernet core service remote IO request. Not a kernel issue - device was in bad state. Should be FREE retry.

Numerical mismatch max diff 6.4375 on TILE_LAYOUT 1x1x32x32. Output has very small values in some columns and large values in others - suggests gamma multiplication is using wrong data (garbage rows from tilize).

hypothesis: ROW broadcast may not just use row 0 - it may use the actual row data. Gamma tilize from 1 stick into c_0 produces tiles where only row 0 has gamma data and rows 1-31 have garbage. If ROW broadcast reads each row individually (not just row 0), it would multiply by garbage for rows 1-31. Need to verify ROW broadcast behavior or fill all 32 rows with gamma data.

approach: Replicate gamma stick 32 times across all rows of c_0 tile-row instead of reading once. ROW broadcast reads per-row data from B tile faces, so all 32 rows must contain gamma values for correct tilize result.

summary: Same max diff 6.4375 after gamma stick replication fix. Output values identical to previous attempt. Need to investigate if gamma multiply is executing at all or if output is from wrong CB.

hypothesis: Output looks like normalize result without gamma. Possible causes: 1) gamma mul not executing, 2) writer reading from wrong CB (c_16 instead of c_4), 3) has_gamma CT arg wrong in writer. Need to check if output matches Stage 3 normalize output exactly. Also need to check kernel cache - maybe old compiled kernel is cached.

approach: Changed Phase 6 gamma multiply from BroadcastDim::ROW to BroadcastDim::NONE. Gamma tiles have all 32 rows filled (replicated stick), so element-wise multiply is correct. ROW broadcast was producing mostly zeros - likely hardware reads only row 0 of B and broadcasts it, but B row 0 in tilized format may not map to the expected data layout.

Added DPRINT debug statements: print c_7 gamma tile after tilize, c_16 normalize output before gamma mul, c_4 gamma mul output. This will show if gamma tilize produces correct data and if the multiply works.
Added unconditional DPRINT at kernel start to verify kernel runs and has_gamma value. Previous DPRINT inside if constexpr blocks never printed - need to confirm has_gamma compile-time value.
Added DPRINT to writer: unconditional print of has_gamma/cb_tile_source/num_rows, and TSLICE of tile data in TILE path after cb_wait_front to see what writer actually reads from the source CB.
Added DPRINT before num_rows==0 check to verify writer actually starts and sees correct CT args
Added detailed NOC address logging (noc_hi/noc_lo/size) to trace where writer sends data

details: Max diff vs gamma_ref 0.09375 - gamma multiply confirmed working after cache clear

approach: Removed all DPRINT debug statements and dprint.h includes from both kernels for clean Stage 4 submission

summary: All CBs balanced for gamma path: c0(reader->tilize), c7(tilize->gamma_mul+manual_pop), c1(reader->square_NoPop+manual_pop), c3(square->reduce), c4(reduce->add then gamma_mul->writer), c5(reader->add_NoPop persisted), c6(add->normalize), c16(normalize->gamma_mul), c17(untilize->writer RM only)

details: numerical_mismatch max_diff=6.4375 on first test case 1x1x32x32 TILE - likely stale kernel cache despite clearing

hypothesis: Output shows reduce pattern (col0 only) suggesting writer reads stale c_4 data from Phase 3 reduce instead of Phase 6 gamma mul output. Possible cause: gamma multiply helper not pushing to c_4 correctly, or the WaitAndPopPerTile policy interacting badly with reused CB. Diagnostic test passed only because extra DPRINT cb_wait_front changed timing.

DPRINT cb_wait_front(cb_out, Wt) before gamma mul was necessary. Without it, WaitAndPopPerTile processes tiles one-at-a-time but something about the pipeline state from Phase 5 COL broadcast interferes. Fix: use WaitUpfrontPopAtEnd for input A in gamma mul so all tiles are waited upfront, matching what DPRINT accidentally provided.

approach: Changed gamma mul input A policy from WaitAndPopPerTile to WaitUpfrontPopAtEnd to ensure all normalize output tiles are present before gamma multiply starts
Reverted to WaitAndPopPerTile + added explicit cb_wait_front(cb_rms,Wt) after gamma mul to ensure pack flush before writer reads

Changed gamma mul BroadcastDim::NONE to BroadcastDim::ROW. ROW broadcast uses row-0 of each B tile face, which matches the replicated gamma layout. This avoids the NONE broadcast LLK path that may have pipeline state issues after COL broadcast in Phase 5.

detail: CB4 reuse across phases 3-4-6 may cause hardware state issue. New approach: use CB8 as temp for normalize output when has_gamma, gamma mul reads CB8*CB7 into CB16. Writer always reads CB16.

approach: New CB8 temp for normalize output when has_gamma. Phase 5 writes to CB8, gamma mul reads CB8*CB7->CB16. Writer always reads CB16. Removed DPRINT. Changed gamma mul input A to WaitAndPopPerTile.
Removed has_gamma conditional for tile source CB. Writer always reads from cb_out (CB16) for TILE path. Removed unused cb_rms constant.

change: CB4 pages back to 2 since gamma output no longer goes to CB4
reason: CB4 no longer reused for gamma multiply output

change: Added CB8 (cb_norm_temp) with Wt pages when has_gamma
reason: New temp CB for normalize output to avoid CB4 reuse across phases

summary: All CBs balanced. New CB8: Phase5 pushes Wt PerTile, gamma mul WaitAndPopPerTile pops Wt. CB16 now always final output: gamma mul pushes Wt or Phase5 pushes Wt. Writer always reads CB16 for TILE path.

detail: Test timed out after 300s. Orchestrator subprocess.TimeoutExpired. No triage log found. Hang likely caused by CB sync issue with new CB8 architecture.
